column36: As a Deputy Editor, Nina Bambysheva leads crypto coverage at Forbes, reporting on everything from industry-defining trends and major deals to regulation. She also co-edits Forbes' 30 under 30, Fintech 50 and Billionaires lists. Since joining Forbes in 2020, she has reported extensively on crypto's most influential founders and enterprises, including Circle, Tether, Strategy and Galaxy. She holds degrees from New York University and Columbia University’s Graduate School of Journalism.

Evan Feng is a Partner and Director of Research at CoinFund, a blockchain-native investment firm founded in 2015. He has spent the last six years investing across DeFi, decentralized AI (DeAI), and web3 gaming. He came to crypto from traditional finance, starting his career at Barclays before moving into fundamental long/short equities investing at Citadel and later Point72, where he covered stocks in the tech, media, and telecom sectors.

Evan got into crypto out of ideological conviction, shaped in part by his family history living under more authoritarian regimes. That history made the idea of trust-minimized, verifiable financial systems feel personal rather than abstract.

He believes the most interesting financial infrastructure of the next decade is being built onchain rather than inside banks, and his work at CoinFund sits at the intersection of DeFi, decentralized AI, and physical implementations of verifiable technology like robotics and defense. As director of research, he brings the same analytical rigor he developed in public markets to how CoinFund evaluates opportunities across the portfolio. Companies Evan has backed at CoinFund include Ondo, Delta Exchange, Gensyn, ether.fi, Perle and Veda.

He writes and speaks on the convergence of traditional and decentralized finance, and is a regular contributor to conversations across DeFi, decentralized AI, and web3 gaming. Outside of work, Evan is an avid gamer across all platforms, into the great outdoors, DIY projects, and sci-fi books. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Finance and Accounting with a minor in Philosophy from New York University.

Jason Jiang is the Chief Business Officer at CertiK. He has a proven executive management track record and over 25 years of experience driving sales growth in industrial, technology, and consumer industries. Before CertiK, he was the Founder and Chief Executive Officer for Inno Optics, Inc., a company that provides RAMAN optical technology-based identification systems to a wide range of industries including food, textile, petroleum, gemstone, and pharmaceutical. 

Jason Jiang has a proven executive management track record and over 15 years of experience driving sales growth in industrial, technology, and consumer industries. He recently served as Chief Operating Officer for Sensimo Inc., a technology company providing AIOT energy-saving solutions to mega Data centers. He was the Founder and Chief Executive Officer for Inno Optics, Inc. from 2012 until 2018, a company that provides RAMAN optical technology-based identification systems to a wide range of industries including food, textile, petroleum, gemstone, and pharmaceutical. The company was publicly listed on China’s NEEQ market in 2018. Prior to joining Inno Optics, Jason was General Manager of China for Greif Inc., a Fortune 500 company, responsible for a business unit’s performance of $100 Million P&L. At Greif China, he led the transformation into China’s top industrial packaging company while growing sales 30% year over year. Previously, he served as founder and VP of Business Development and Operation of AEC-MotionStar, Inc. Under his guidance, he grew the company from a start-up stage to $4M in revenue within 3 years and one factory operation. Jason spent the prior 8 years at Motorola, holding various engineering and management positions.

Jody Mettler is the COO & President of BitGo Bank & Trust, National Association and is responsible for managing BitGo Trust companies to meet high standards of customer care and to ensure the safe and secure custody of their assets. Prior to joining BitGo Trust, Jody was the Director of Global Transformation at Citicorp. She spent over 20 years with Citicorp in various capacities ranging from corporate accounting to operations management primarily within the Institutional Client Group servicing large corporations to develop payment facilitation methods. Jody also served as a member of the Board of Directors for Citicorp South Dakota Trust Company.

Jody is currently a member of the South Dakota Trust Association and a member of the digital asset legislative task force to facilitate trust administration digital asset laws.

column36: Co-founder and CEO of Blockstream, Dr. Adam Back, invented Hashcash, the proof-of-work algorithm cited by Satoshi Nakamoto in the Bitcoin whitepaper, as the future basis for its mining function. Throughout his two-decade-long vocation as an applied cryptographer and security architect, he has held senior roles with a number of technology companies, including Microsoft, EMC, PI, VMware, and Zero-Knowledge Systems, as well as advised many more companies on cryptography and peer-to-peer finance. Dr. Adam Back holds a computer science Ph.D. in distributed systems from the University of Exeter.

column5: Global Head of Innovation and Digital Assets
column36:
As Global Head of Innovation and Digital Assets at Euroclear, I lead initiatives focused on how tokenization, distributed ledger technologies, and digital assets can support the evolution of financial market infrastructure. My work centers on enabling the institutional adoption of digital asset securities and advancing the role of digital currencies and stablecoins within trusted financial systems.

Much of this work involves collaborating across the industry to help build the foundations of the next generation of financial infrastructure. It also includes contributing to broader industry initiatives and collaborations aimed at advancing the development of digital asset infrastructure across global capital markets.

In this role, I work closely with financial institutions, market infrastructures, technology providers, and regulators to advance initiatives that support secure, interoperable, and scalable digital asset ecosystems. I am also actively involved in industry discussions and collaborative initiatives aimed at shaping common standards for digital asset market infrastructure.

Beyond my executive role, I contribute to several industry initiatives. I serve as Founding Chairman of the Board and current Co-Chairman of the Canton Network Foundation, supporting the development and governance of the Canton ecosystem. I am also a member of the FinTech & Digitalisation Advisory Committee of the International Capital Market Association, contributing to discussions on the impact of innovation and technology on global capital markets, and an Ambassador of the Global Blockchain Business Council, supporting dialogue on the development of digital asset ecosystems.

Earlier in my career, I held several leadership roles in international retail banking, focusing on mergers and acquisitions, restructuring, and digital transformation. I also served as CEO of two BNP Paribas subsidiaries in Morocco, DIGIFI, a regulated payment service provider, and DIGISERV, a digital factory, which I created and led as CEO as part of a broader effort to expand access to digital financial services in the region.

Throughout my career, I have led transformation initiatives across different markets and regulatory environments, working with diverse teams and partners to help bridge traditional financial infrastructure with emerging digital financial systems.

I also hold a PhD in Law, reflecting a long-standing interest in financial regulation, market infrastructure, and innovation.

Founder of Crunch, pioneer of decentralized AI. Crunch is a Decentralized AI research lab powered by 11,000+ AI experts and 1,200+ PhDs across 100+ countries, forming a distributed global brain. The network builds high impact predictive models in quantitative finance, genomics, supply chain, and energy, tackling complex predictive algorithmic systems at scale.

Fabian is Chief Investment Officer, leading the team responsible for developing Sygnum’s institutional investment strategies and growing portfolio of passive and active asset management products.

Before joining Sygnum, Fabian held various executive positions at Notenstein La Roche Private Bank and 1741 Asset Management. Prior to that, he worked for Wegelin & Co. Private Bankers in numerous positions.

Fabian holds a Master of Arts in Quantitative Economics and Finance and a Bachelor of Arts in Economics from the University of St. Gallen.

Kathleen Wrynn is Global Head of Digital Assets at Invesco. In this role, she leads the global
strategy and execution for our digital asset initiatives. That includes our roadmap for tokenized
products, crypto-linked offerings in traditional wrappers, and the blockchain applications that
support future innovation.
Ms. Wrynn joined Invesco in 2025. Prior to joining the firm, she served as a strategy lead for
the distributed ledger technologies product line in the Chief Technology Office at
JPMorganChase, supporting strategic blockchain initiatives. Before that, she was a senior
global digital products manager at DWS, leading product strategy and development for digital
asset initiatives. Earlier in her career, she spent over 13 years at Deutsche Bank in various
roles across multiple divisions. Ms. Wrynn entered the financial services industry in 2008.
Ms. Wrynn earned a BA degree in international and global studies from Brandeis University and
an MA in international economics and finance from Brandeis International Business School.
She is an Amazon Web Services (AWS) Certified Cloud Practitioner and holds multiple
certifications from the Blockchain Council, including Certified Blockchain Expert, Certified
Quorum Developer, Certified in Decentralized Finance (DeFi), and Certified in Non-Fungible
Tokens (NFT). Additionally, Ms. Wrynn is a Certified Enterprise Blockchain Professional (CEBP).

Julien Clausse serves as the Head of AssetFoundry at BNP Paribas CIB, the department working on Digital Assets & Tokenization across CIB’s businesses, and beyond in the Group. For the last 25+ years, as a Venture Builder, he has created and launched new products in several industries – from automotive, to TravelTech, to Internet of Things and now in Finance when he joined BNP Paribas CIB in 2018.

Julien oversees AssetFoundry's commitment to financial instrument tokenization, aiming to assist both issuers and investors by integrating issuances with key asset data, such as ESG metrics, as well as reaping the benefits of this new business architecture based on blockchain rails. AssetFoundry department also works on blockchain networks’ connectivity and safekeeping of private keys in a constantly evolving market.

Guided by Julien's direction, the AssetFoundry team closely work with all business lines, as well as departments like legal, IT, and compliance, towards a seamless originate-to-distribute-to-custody platform, holistically including operations, technology, legal framework and regulations.

Luca Burlando leads Republic.com’s infrastructure arm, operating nodes for 25+ chains. Previously he was Head of Ventures & Partnerships at Sygnum, investing in digital asset companies across Europe and APAC. With previous experiences at Credit Suisse and Société Générale, Luca has an extensive experience across private and public markets in Europe and APAC.

Dr. Martin Derka is a distinguished figure in the blockchain space, with an extensive background in the development of smart contracts and Ethereum-based platforms. His work is particularly noted for its significant contributions to DeFi, where he has specialized in enhancing security measures and mitigating economic manipulations. As a co-founder of Zircuit, he has been instrumental in advancing the state of scalability and privacy in blockchain technologies. Martin's leadership in the design and implementation of cutting-edge rollup solutions has positioned him as a key influencer in the Ethereum ecosystem.

Josiah is a repeat founder, operator and former lawyer, having studied law at the LSE, Harvard and Oxford. Aged 20, his legal work was cited by the New Zealand Supreme Court and published in Europe's no.1 law journal, earning him a place as the youngest Kennedy Scholar at Harvard in over a decade. After winning 15+ scholarships and prizes, he pivoted from law and exited his first startup at 21 within 8 months.

He joined Teya as their 12th employee and over 3 years helped scale the business to 2,000+ people across 25+ countries and a $9.6B valuation, holding roles including Chief of Staff, Country Manager and Head of Public Policy. He was previously COO at Ontik, a B2B financial operations platform. Named Entrepreneur of the Year 2025 by the Sutton Trust, he's now CEO & Co-Founder of Zuba, building stablecoin-native payments infrastructure after spending months on the ground in Lagos, Nairobi and Cape Town understanding the broken systems holding back cross-border trade.

Sun is the co-founder and CEO of Veda, the leading vault infrastructure firm that allows neobanks, fintechs, exchanges, and institutions to integrate multichain, multiprotocol yield products. He is also a co-founder of Seven Seas Capital and has a background in physics and machine learning research, Sun is now focused on bringing institutions onchain.

column36: Tero Reuna is the CEO of Paxos Issuance Europe and the Head of Growth for EMEA at Paxos. Prior to joining Paxos, Tero held various leadership positions at Tietoevry, including Head of Global Accounts, where he was responsible for the SaaS business's international expansion. Tero’s earlier career includes pivotal roles at Hewlett Packard Enterprise and IBM. Tero holds a Master’s degree in Economics and Business from the Turku School of Economics.

As Head of Digital Assets Research and Portfolio Manager of the VanEck OnChain Economy ETF ($NODE), Mr. Sigel helps guide VanEck’s digital assets strategy and sits on the investment committee of the firm’s private funds. He produces frequent research on Bitcoin, smart contract platforms, and emerging markets adoption of digital asset. Prior to VanEck, Mr. Sigel  worked as a research analyst and portfolio strategist at CLSA, an analyst and portfolio manager at AllianceBernstein (where he covered technology and other sectors under Cathie Wood), and a journalist at Bloomberg, CNBC and NHK Japan Broadcasting, where he covered finance. Mr. Sigel is a CFA Charterholder and has a BA from Harvard University

As a Capital Markets Technology Manager at Google, Moritz Platt is responsible for go-to-market for the Google Cloud Universal Ledger in EMEA. He partners with clients across capital markets, banking, and financial market infrastructure to thoughtfully apply DLT concepts to highly-regulated industries. Prior to joining Google, Moritz built deep expertise in distributed ledger technology, driving institutional DLT adoption through key customer-facing roles at both R3 and AWS. He holds a PhD from King's College London, where he continues his research on blockchain consensus mechanisms as a Visiting Fellow.

column36:
With over 15 years of leadership experience, Yana Prikhodchenko has built a dynamic career with international companies across diverse sectors, including insurance and banking. Known for navigating complex, fast-moving industries, she has proven herself a trusted executive and strategic force in every organization she has led.
For the last 4 years, she has been at the forefront of the cryptocurrency market, leading Cointelegraph Global as CEO,  transforming it from a news site into a full ecosystem and redefining what a media company can be in the Web3 era. Her strategic vision and deep market expertise have scaled the company into a multi-vertical powerhouse.

Under her leadership, Cointelegraph Global now encompasses Cointelegraph Media, Formula Marketing Agency, Cointelegraph Accelerator, LONGITUDE event and Cointelegraph Research, among others, making it one of the most influential ecosystems in the global crypto space.

Yiannis is the founder of Five North, a firm building institutional infrastructure for tokenized markets and operating as a Canton Network Super Validator. He serves on the board of the Canton Foundation and works across blockchain governance, financing applications, and real-world asset issuance. A former Silicon Valley engineer and repeat entrepreneur, Yiannis now backs and builds ventures at the intersection of fintech, data, crypto, and regulated digital markets.

Jean‑Thomas Ledore is a Partner at PwC Strategy&, based in Paris, where he advises global leaders on strategic transformation, value creation, and large‑scale operating model redesign at the intersection of industry, digital, and emerging technologies.

With more than 15 years of experience, Jean‑Thomas combines a rare dual background as both a strategy consultant and a senior corporate executive. He spent six years as a COMEX member of a €2bn consumer goods group, successively serving as Head of Strategy & M&A, CFO, CIO, and Head of Legal, leading complex transformations, governance redesign, and value‑accretive transactions. He later joined Strategy& where he is now a Partner, and the Global Client Partner for CMA CGM, one of the world’s leading shipping and logistics groups.

Jean‑Thomas has led numerous large‑scale AI, digital, and data‑driven transformation programs across transportation & logistics, consumer goods, retail, and family‑owned businesses. His work includes group‑level digital and AI strategies, R&D and innovation roadmaps, deal and synergy structuring, and post‑merger value capture, often at global scale and under demanding governance environments.

More recently, his attention has increased to blockchain technologies and specifically Decentralized AI, exploring how these Web3 architectures can unlock new forms of trust, efficiency, and value creation for enterprises—from tokenized assets and data monetization to decentralized intelligence networks and next‑generation operating models.

column5: Director on BlackRock's Digital Assets team
column36:
Nikhil is a Director in the Digital Assets team at BlackRock, overseeing international digital assets initiatives, where he partners with internal business units and drives implementation of products focused on cryptoassets, stablecoins and tokenized assets.
Prior to BlackRock, Nikhil was one of the founding members of Kinexys by J.P. Morgan (formerly Onyx), the firm's dedicated blockchain business unit. As a Senior Product Manager, he designed and commercialized several blockchain solutions from concept to market. Before J.P. Morgan, Nikhil was with SinoPac Securities Asia Ltd., structuring lifecycle solutions for alternative investment funds.
Nikhil is a patent-holding inventor, has co-authored numerous thought-papers on tokenization in capital markets and actively participates in industry working groups shaping standards and operating models in the space. He combines over a decade of traditional finance expertise with deep blockchain innovation, bringing a practitioner's perspective on scaling tokenization within regulated environments. An Electronics and Telecommunications engineer by training, he holds an MBA from the University of Hong Kong and London Business School.

Andy Tang is a Partner at Draper Associates, where he focuses on investing in early-stage technology companies and
guiding their growth, strategic development, and partnerships. He is also a Founding Partner at Draper Dragon and
serves as Chairman of the Executive Committee at Draper University, a Silicon Valley accelerator and entrepreneurship
education program where he teaches finance and fundraising to aspiring entrepreneurs.
In addition, Andy directs VCx, an invite-only alliance of family offices that promotes generational relationship building,
education, and co-investing. VCx now includes over 50 member families across the globe, whose core businesses span
retail, real estate, food and beverage, insurance, mining and resources, industrial, transportation, and banking sectors.
With over two decades of experience spanning operations, R&D, investment banking, and venture investing, Andy has
played a pivotal role in seeding over 20 unicorns and several decacorns, including Polymarket, Colossal, and Oklo.
His current investment interests include Bleeding Edge Technology (BET), AI infrastructure, Digital Health, and Digital
Assets/Web3. His portfolio features notable technology unicorns and decacorns such as Oklo** (NYSE: OKLO),
Polymarket**, Colossal**, NovelBeam* (SSE: 688677), Otter.ai*, Splashtop*, Yeepay*, Jing Jin Electric* (SSE: 688280),
AltoBeam, Vicarious Artificial Intelligence, Broadbus (MOT), Unstoppable Domains, and 1047 Games*.
Andy has also led and managed major digital investments in companies such as Carv ($CARV), Galxe* ($GAL), EtherFi*
($EETH), HighStreet ($HIGH), Arkham* ($ARKM), VeChain* ($VET), Tezos ($XTZ), Certik* ($CTK), QuarkChain* ($QKC),
Ripio* ($RPC), Ledger, Coinbase, QTUM*, OASIS* ($ROSE), IOTEX*, Gemini, Lemon Cash, and CoinDCX, among others.
His recent Bleeding Edge Technology investments include Gravitics, Firehawk, Outpost, MaxSpace, Slingshot, Venus
Aerospace, and Iceye*, while his Digital Health investments include Clinchoice*, EDDA*, Atomwise, Verge Genomics,
Ten63 Therapeutics, Imprimed, and Pretty Litter.
Before joining the Draper investment platform in 2006, Andy was a partner at ABB Technologies Ventures and Infineon
Ventures. Earlier in his career, he worked in investment banking with Credit Suisse First Boston’s technology group in
Palo Alto, where he contributed to the Magma IPO (SNPS) and the Cyras (CIEN) acquisition under the leadership of Frank
Quattrone and George Boutros. Before banking, Andy served as a senior engineer and marketing manager at Intel,
focusing on chip design and market enabling for the Pentium and Itanium processors.
Originally from Taiwan, Andy moved to Arlington, Texas at age 13. He holds degrees from UT Austin (B.S. in Electrical &
Computer Engineering), MIT (M.S. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science) under the guidance of President
Rafael Reif, and Wharton (MBA). He also holds a U.S. patent in silicon germanium thin film materials and was honored
as a Distinguished Young Alumni by Texas Governor Greg Abbott.
Andy lives in Menlo Park, California, with his wife Emily and their two sons. He is an avid skier, animal lover, and
dedicated meditation practitioner.
** Denotes decacorn companies seeded
* Denotes unicorn companies seeded

I am Matthew Felice Pace, the Chief Executive Officer of Spectrum Nodes, a leading engineering company specialising in delivering secure, reliable, and high-performance solutions in the blockchain space.
 
Since co-founding the company in 2018, we have been core infrastructure providers for some of the leading Web3 protocols including Chainlink and Lido, helping secure billions of dollars in value and transactions across 20+ networks.
 
As the space evolved and blockchain technology is increasingly being adopted by traditional enterprises, fintech companies and many others, we have leveraged our expertise to build Spectrum with the mission to provide blockchain connectivity for developers and companies via the same clean developer ergonomics they have come to expect when building awesome Web2 products.
 
After only a few years we are already serving billions of requests daily and cannot wait for the coming age of AI, agents, robotics, wearables etc all running on blockchain rails to ensure verifiable, private, secure, permissionless, trustworthy products the space was built to deliver.
 
I am also actively involved in the global startup ecosystem, mentoring founders, supporting early-stage innovation, and participating in internationally recognised startup competitions across blockchain, AI, and emerging technologies. My focus is on bridging the gap between ambitious founders and the infrastructure required to build scalable, blockchain enable real-world products.
 
When I am not building you can find me training for some crazy fitness competition, reading up on my Dostoyevsky or the universe and its mysteries.

- Am serving as the CEO of LDC EU (HK listed payment giant LianLian Global’s web3 daughter in Europe) and am building out MiCAR and PSD2 for them 
- Served as the COO and MD of KuCoin EU and built out the EU platform for the top-10 exchange; recently have re-built and scaled as well another European based exchange Blocktrade as the CEO 
- Hands-on Product and Operations Executive with 16y Banking and PE experience
- Massive experience in transforming bank buinsess models through Asset/Liability Sales restructuring programs and business model changes (re-built with Cerberus HSH Nordbank from the worst bank in Germany to the most profitable German bank within 4 years)
- Initiated the Fintech Advisory Board of the Austrian Finance Ministry back in 2018 and co-founded the DAAA (Digital Asset Association Austria) back in 2018 as well
- Seasoned early stage Fintech and Digital Asset investor (100+ investments) either personally or through our fund smape.capital (Co-Founder and GP)
- Built as well a node operator / staking service provider company called daic.capital
- Acting as well as a Senior Advisor to regulated and unregulated companies


- Privately I have a passion for travelling (100+ countries), football and skiing

Dr. Suzanne Morsfield is CFO and Global Head of Accounting & Tax Solutions for Lukka, a global software and data company serving institutions and governments engaged with crypto assets. She brings more than 25 years of data-driven international experience to her role as a thought leader in corporate reporting and regulation, currently focusing on digital assets. 


In addition, Suzanne has been the Policy Advisor for CryptoUK, the UK’s leading industry association.She has held sell-side and buy-side analysis roles at Morgan Stanley and JP Morgan, respectively. Suzanne was an auditor at EY and also served as the Director of Research at Columbia Business School’s think tank, the Center for Excellence in Accounting and Security Analysis (CEASA). Suzanne joined the IFRS Foundation in 2015, where she led projects on improving the primary IFRS financial statements and related disclosures. She has served on committees for both the Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) and the IFRS Foundation, and was a member of the World Economic Forum’s Crypto Sustainability Coalition and its related Energy Usage Working Group. 


Suzanne's academic experience includes contributions to London School of Economics, New York University, and other US universities. She holds a PhD from the University of Arizona and has won numerous teaching and research awards. She regularly writes and speaks about regulatory developments, and on financial reporting and tax matters as they relate to crypto and digital assets.

Johannes Kern is the Managing Director of the Frankencoin Association, the non-profit organization stewarding the largest Swiss franc stablecoin. He works at the point where DeFi meets institutional finance, with a focus on stablecoin design, CDP mechanics, and Swiss regulatory frameworks. Before Frankencoin, he helped build one of the largest digital assets allocators in Europe and worked within a single family office. He holds an M.A. in Management from the University of St. Gallen.

Joris Delanoue is the Founder & CEO of Fairmint, the 1st SEC-registered transfer agent bringing equity natively onchain by turning cap tables into smart contracts. He also sits on the Board of Directors of the Canton Foundation, where he was elected General Director. After exiting his previous ventures, Joris is now leading the convergence of Wall Street and blockchain, with Fairmint having already moved $1.5B+ of equity onchain while co-authoring the Open Cap Table Protocol now shaping the industry's standards.

column5: Executive Director of Financial Market Infrastructure
Managing Director | Head of Issuer Services & New Digital Markets
column36:
Jens Hachmeister serves as a Managing Director at Deutsche Börse Group, bringing over 25 years of experience in various senior positions within the Capital Markets and Financial Services industry. Currently, Jens is the Head of Issuer Services and New Digital Markets, a Member of the Clearstream Management Board. Additionally, he holds several board positions in different FinTech companies and is a member of the Digital Council of the German Central Bank. Jens also served as a member of the FinTech Advisory Committee to the German Ministry of Finance for two legislative periods. Jens joined the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF) Board of Directors in July 2025.

In 2018, Jens established the New Digital Markets Area as a key pillar of Deutsche Börse’s digital, DLT, and blockchain strategy. He took on additional responsibility as Clearstream’s Head of Issuer Services in February 2020. Before driving Deutsche Börse’s digital transformation, Jens held numerous senior management positions within the Deutsche Börse Group, including Chief HR Officer, Head of Strategic Execution, and Chief of Staff to the CEO of the Group. Jens has extensive experience in capital market infrastructure design, build-out, and operations along the entire value chain of the industry. His previous roles at Deutsche Börse included leading large transformational market infrastructure projects and managing customer support and market operations departments for the trading venues and the Clearing House of Deutsche Börse Group. Prior to joining Deutsche Börse Group, Jens was a management consultant at KPMG.

Nikola Stojanow is an entrepreneur, venture capitalist, and blockchain pioneer with deep experience building and scaling global technologies. He is currently Partner, New Ventures at Bank Frick and Director at Portal to Bitcoin Foundation. He co-founded æternity, one of the earliest scalable Layer 1 protocols, and later led the Aeternity Foundation, driving ecosystem growth, strategic investments, and international partnerships. His current work spans institutional financial infrastructure, agentic AI systems, and research at the intersection of blockchain, payments, and autonomous agents.

Managing Partner of STORM Partners, leading professional services firm dedicated to the blockchain industry, and Founder of recently launched innovation centre, Decentral House, in Geneva, Switzerland - Sheraz Ahmed is on a mission to provide the emerging technology industry with the necessary tools to succeed in this fast-growing digital age.

The serial entrepreneur is a Global Shaper of the World Economic Forum, supporting dialogue, action and collaborative change. Sheraz also drives cooperation and integrity across the Web3 space as Ecosystem Director of the Crypto Valley Association.

column5: Head of Emerging Industries and Digital Assets
column36:
Head of Emerging Industries and Digital Assets, Alera Group

Shane Smith leads the Emerging Industries and Digital Assets vertical at Alera Group, where he focuses on building insurance structures, risk transfer strategies, and technology-enabled programs for next generation industries.

Alera Group is one of the largest insurance and financial services firms in the country, with $1.6 billion in annual gross revenue and more than 4,600 colleagues. Alera Group is ranked #14 on Business Insurance’s Top 100 Brokers of U.S. Business and has been listed among the Top Global Insurance Brokers by Best’s Review. Through Alera’s scale, national platform, and market access, Shane and his team are able to support complex and emerging risks across industries that are rapidly evolving beyond traditional insurance models.

His work spans the full innovation supply chain, from Rocks to Rockets. This includes critical minerals, power generation, data centers, AI and digital assets, advanced manufacturing, and the space economy.

Shane architects new insurance programs for industries that are often ahead of traditional underwriting models. His focus is on translating complex emerging risks into structured insurance solutions that support financing, commercialization, and long-term resilience.

At the center of Shane’s work is the belief that insurance is not just a back-end requirement. It is a critical layer of infrastructure that enables innovation to scale. By combining insurance expertise, emerging technology, predictive modeling, and new software architecture, Shane is focused on building the risk infrastructure needed to insure the future by ensuring the future.

Alex is the founder and CEO of Upbit Global, which operates Upbit’s international digital asset businesses across Singapore, Thailand, and Indonesia. He also founded VerifyVASP the world’s largest Travel Rule solution, supporting over 180 digital asset service providers globally. Through active engagement with international organizations and regulatory authorities, he has contributed to the development of global digital asset regulatory frameworks.

Mohamed Ezeldin is the Head of Animoca Labs at Animoca Brands￼, where he leads the company’s next-generation initiatives at the intersection of AI, digital assets, gamified finance, and decentralized technology.

Previously the founder and head of Animoca Brands’ Tokenomics division, Mohamed has spent years designing token economies, governance systems, incentive structures, and growth strategies for some of the most recognized projects across Web3, gaming, and digital identity. His work has helped shape how blockchain ecosystems evolve beyond speculation into sustainable, user-driven economies.

Today, his primary focus is building Minds by Animoca Brands￼ — a human-centric AI ecosystem exploring persistent memory, AI identity, agent coordination, and the future relationship between humans and intelligent systems. Through Animoca Labs, he is leading efforts to rethink how AI products are experienced, monetized, and integrated into everyday workflows, with a strong emphasis on accessibility, usability, and digital ownership.

Before entering Web3, Mohamed spent a decade as a mathematics educator and Head of Mathematics, a background that continues to influence his systems-oriented approach to technology, behavior, and economic design.

Mohamed regularly speaks on AI, digital ownership, tokenized ecosystems, decentralized governance, and the future of human-AI interaction, combining deep theoretical thinking with hands-on product and operational experience.

Maha Al-Saadi is a regulatory affairs and compliance leader with 19 years of international experience across Europe and the Middle East. As Head of Regulatory Affairs at Qatar Financial Centre Authority (QFC), she shapes regulatory frameworks that balance market integrity with innovation spanning financial services, digital assets, AI governance, and emerging market structures.
Her career spans senior roles at Qatar Financial Centre Regulatory Authoirty, Deutsche Börse Group, Trade Republic Bank, and Bankhaus Scheich, advising executive leadership and engaging regulators on complex cross-border policy. She is known for bridging traditional finance and digital innovation, helping institutions navigate evolving requirements while enabling responsible adoption of new technologies,  an approach informed by her LLM in Finance Law from Goethe University's Institute of Law and Finance.

Tim is the Co-founder and CTO of Score and Manako. He leads the technical development of Subnet 44 on Bittensor, building the incentive layers and infrastructure necessary for decentralised computer vision at scale. Beyond the network, Tim drives the productisation of these technologies at Manako, turning Bittensor’s raw intelligence into production-ready vision systems for real-world use cases.

column5: Global Head of Cash & Digital Assets

column36: Gavin Zaentz is the co-founder and CEO of Leadpoet, a Bittensor-powered AI agent network redefining B2B sales. Previously at Nasdaq, he worked on equities trading infrastructure products. Gavin brings hands-on sales and growth experience from building a prior startup and helping double AUM through hedge fund capital formation. He is passionate about using decentralized AI to make demand generation more scalable, effective, and outcome-driven.

Rena Shah is the President & COO of the Stacks Endowment, the long-term treasury for the Stacks Ecosystem, where she invests in Bitcoin DeFi. She previously was COO of Trust Machines, a Bitcoin product studio, where she contributed to Granite (Bitcoin Lending Protocol) and Leather (Bitcoin Wallet).

Prior to that, Rena was Head of Exchange at Binance.US, where she grew the exchange from $30M to $3B in daily trading volume within 18 months.

Rena has a distinctive background spanning commodities and energy — she began her career as a drilling engineer working offshore on drilling rigs, while simultaneously spinning up Bitcoin mining pools.

Chi Zhang is the Co-founder and CEO of Kite AI, building the base layer for the agentic internet. Her extensive background encompasses AI, big data, and product management. Chi successfully spearheaded data engineering product development at Databricks and played a key role as a founding AI leader at dotData, an Automated Machine Learning platform. Chi got her Ph.D. in ML/AI (Statistics) and M.A. in Economics from UC Berkeley. Outside of her professional achievements,she mentors early-stage founders through StartX, Stanford’s startup accelerator.

Alice Li is a Partner at Foresight Ventures, where she focuses on accelerating consumer adoption of Web3 through investments in stablecoin infrastructure, payment systems, AI applications, and social media platforms. Prior to Foresight, she honed her expertise in product growth at TikTok and Amazon. She bridges Web2 execution strategies with Web3 innovation to scale blockchain solutions for mainstream users, with an expertise to identify early stage consumer-friendly startups.

Itai Elizur is the Co-Founder and Managing Partner at MarketAcross, and Partner at InboundJunction and Chainwire.

MarketAcross is the Web3 branch of the InboundJunction group and has served over 300 blockchain companies, including Binance, Solana, Polygon, Crypto.com, Polkadot, Tezos, Avalanche, Kucoin, and eToro - to name a few.

Itai has helped media groups, enterprise companies, and startups develop and execute 360° marketing and growth strategies in the digital space.

Itai has worked in content, creative, and marketing roles at Wix, Infolinks, and The Forward. Itai is also an angel investor, investing at BlockBuilders, and was a mentor at 500Global’s Tel Aviv office

Steven Goldfeder, Co-Founder and CEO of Offchain, co-founded the company in 2013 and worked as an initial developer of Arbitrum, one of the largest and most advanced blockchains in the Ethereum ecosystem. Steven authored "Bitcoin and Cryptocurrency Technologies" and pioneered multi-party computation, shaping the future of blockchain interaction for consumers and institutions alike. Steven received his doctorate from Princeton University and is a published author of multiple research papers on blockchain science, crypto technologies, smart contracts, and more
